input.document_text:
Project Brief: AI-Powered Student Workflow Manager

Objective:
Build a web-based system using Z.AI GLM as the reasoning engine to help
student groups manage project workflows. The system must support document
ingestion, automated task planning, role assignment, and risk detection.

Deliverables:
1. Working web prototype (React frontend + FastAPI backend)
2. System architecture document with component diagram
3. 3000-word technical report covering design decisions
4. 10-minute demonstration video
5. QA testing document

Evaluation Criteria:
- System Architecture & Design: 30%
- Code Quality & Modularity: 25%
- AI Integration Depth: 20%
- Documentation: 15%
- Presentation: 10%

Deadline: 1 December 2025
Team size: 4 members

deliverables.title: Working Web Prototype
deliverables.description: React frontend + FastAPI backend implementing document ingestion, automated task planning, role assignment, and risk detection powered by Z.AI GLM

deliverables.title: System Architecture Document
deliverables.description: Architecture document with component diagram detailing system design and component interactions

deliverables.title: Technical Report
deliverables.description: 3000-word report covering design decisions and rationale

deliverables.title: QA Testing Document
deliverables.description: Testing documentation covering quality assurance processes and results

grading_priorities.notes: Highest weight; architecture document and prototype design must be thorough and well-reasoned
Second highest; codebase should be clean, well-structured, and modular
Z.AI GLM integration must go beyond surface-level calls; demonstrate meaningful use of the reasoning engine
Covers architecture doc, technical report, and QA document quality
Demo video quality and clarity of communication

implicit_expectations: Z.AI GLM must be meaningfully integrated as the reasoning engine, not just a superficial API call
All four core features (document ingestion, task planning, role assignment, risk detection) must be functional in the prototype
The system is designed for student group use, implying multi-user or collaborative considerations
The architecture document should clearly show how Z.AI GLM fits into the system
The technical report should justify technology choices and design patterns
The QA document should cover both frontend and backend testing
Code modularity suggests the team should follow separation of concerns and clean architecture principles
Team of 4 implies need for clear division of work and coordinated integration

ambiguities.suggested_clarification: Specify which document formats must be supported (PDF, DOCX, plain text, etc.) and expected ingestion volume
Define what constitutes 'deep' integration vs. surface-level use — e.g., multi-step reasoning, context retention, chaining prompts
Clarify what types of risks — project timeline risks, task dependency risks, team member workload risks, or all of the above
Determine if role assignment should be AI-suggested, manual, or a hybrid approach with AI recommendations
Specify whether unit tests, integration tests, end-to-end tests, or user acceptance testing are expected and at what coverage level
Clarify if the prototype needs to be deployed to a hosting environment or if local execution is sufficient for evaluation

structured_goals.statement: Design and implement a robust system architecture for the AI-Powered Student Workflow Manager with clear component separation
Build a functional React + FastAPI web prototype with all four core features: document ingestion, automated task planning, role assignment, and risk detection
Achieve deep integration with Z.AI GLM reasoning engine across multiple system features
Ensure code quality and modularity through clean architecture, separation of concerns, and consistent coding standards
Produce a comprehensive system architecture document with component diagram
Write a 3000-word technical report thoroughly covering design decisions and rationale
Create a QA testing document covering test strategies and results for both frontend and backend
Record a 10-minute demonstration video clearly showcasing system capabilities
Coordinate team of 4 members with clear division of responsibilities to meet 1 December 2025 deadline

result.source_notes: Project brief document; no speakers to identify. Key sections: Objective, Deliverables (5 items), Evaluation Criteria (5 categories with weights), Deadline and team size constraints.
